---
title: "Colter_Bay_Red_White"
author: "Nigel K. Anderson"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
1 + 1
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
#| echo: false
2 * 2
```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).

## Practicing Load Data

First I had to create a shortcut on the shared folder in google drive to be on mydrive. I then found that shared folder on mydrive in finder and copied the location url.

```{r}

df <- readxl::read_excel("/Users/nanderson/Library/CloudStorage/GoogleDrive-nigel_anderson@brown.edu/.shortcut-targets-by-id/1RxI5D6hPL6E8DyVwvePE0g8a1CvV1L9D/grandteton_colterbay/grte_colterbay_2019/grte_amla1_2019/grte_amla1_06_18_2019/Data_CumulativeSonoBatch_v4.4.5.xlsx")

clean <- df %>%
  filter(Prob > 0.9)

summary <- clean %>%
  group_by(SppAccp) %>%
  summarise(n = n())

print(summary)
```

Here I summarize the number of times different species show up in a single Sonobat recording. This one is for site AMLA on 06/18/2019.

```{r}

files <- list.files(
  path = "/Users/nanderson/Library/CloudStorage/GoogleDrive-nigel_anderson@brown.edu/.shortcut-targets-by-id/1RxI5D6hPL6E8DyVwvePE0g8a1CvV1L9D/grandteton_colterbay",
  pattern = "\\.xlsx$",
  full.names = TRUE,
  recursive = TRUE
)

```

```{r}

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)

process_file <- function(file) {
  df <- tryCatch(
    readxl::read_excel(file),
    error = function(e) return(NULL)
  )
  
  if (is.null(df)) return(NULL)
  
  if(!"Prob" %in% names(df)) return(NULL)
  
  df_clean <- df %>%
    filter(Prob >= 0.9)
  
  df_clean %>%
    group_by(SppAccp) %>%
    summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
    mutate(file = basename(file))
}

all_counts <- map_dfr(files, process_file)
```
